draft: Hi Maya, as a fellow Columbia alum I was struck by your move from consulting into climate product at Helio Grid — that's exactly the path I'm exploring after several years in strategy. I've been trying to understand how people actually break into climate product work. Would you be open to a 20-minute call in the next couple of weeks to share how you navigated that switch? Either way, thank you for the inspiration.

Hi Dana, Priya Shah suggested I reach out — she spoke highly of your work on Stripe's payments reliability team. I'm a backend engineer fascinated by how you keep latency low at that scale. Could I borrow 15 minutes to hear what the day-to-day actually looks like on your team? Completely understand if now's a busy stretch.
note: Warm referral open, concrete detail, easy to decline.

draft: Hi Sam, we overlapped briefly at Datalux before you left for Wayfinder Health, and I've followed your shift into health data with real admiration. I'm weighing a similar move out of ad-tech analytics. Would you have 20 minutes to tell me what surprised you most about working on clinical data? No worries at all if your plate is full.
note: Shared history, specific curiosity, respectful ask.

draft: Hi Priya, fellow Columbia alum here — I noticed Northwind Energy's new community-solar program and your part in scaling its operations. I'm trying to learn how operations folks actually get into the energy transition. Might you be free for a short call to share how you found your way there? Thanks so much for considering it.
note: Specific program reference, clear small ask.

draft: Hi Alex, we were in the same Ironhack design cohort a couple of years back — I've loved watching your product design work at Lumen Labs since. I'm exploring how designers move into AI products and would value your view. Would you be up for a quick 15-minute chat about what that transition was like? Totally fine if not.
note: Genuine shared background, tailored, optional ask.

draft: Hi Jordan, as a Columbia alum I was glad to see your name leading risk analytics at Cedar Bank. I'm an analyst trying to understand how quantitative risk teams actually operate day to day. Could I steal 20 minutes to hear how you built your path into that work? Grateful either way — your story is encouraging.
note: Warm, specific to the recipient's role, humble ask.

draft: Hi there, fellow alum here. I really admire your company and everything your team does. I'm exploring new directions in my career and would love to connect with someone like you. Would you be open to a quick chat sometime soon? Thanks so much, looking forward to hearing from you.
note: Passes the gate but generic — could be pasted to anyone. Low personalization.

draft: Hi Dana, fellow Columbia alum here. I'll be honest — I think I'd be a fantastic fit for your team and could add value from day one. I'm available to start immediately and I'm confident I'd ramp fast. Could we set up a call so I can walk you through what I'd bring? Let me know what works for you.
note: Subtle selling the word-filter misses: self-promotion + availability push. Low non_salesy.

draft: Dear Mr. Sam, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to humbly introduce myself as a fellow Datalux acquaintance. I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this message may cause you. I would be most grateful and deeply honored if you might possibly consider granting me a brief moment of your valuable time? Thank you ever so much.
note: Stiff, grovelling, over-formal. Low tone_conciseness and warmth.

draft: Hi Priya, so I was sort of thinking, as a Columbia alum and all, that maybe, if it's not too much trouble, I might possibly reach out, because I've kind of been wondering about energy and operations and things like that lately, and I guess I just wanted to see whether, you know, you'd maybe be around to chat at some point? Anyway, thanks I suppose.
note: Under 100 words but rambling and hedgy. Low conciseness.

draft: Hi Alex, I'm a designer with five years of experience and a strong portfolio looking for my next step. I've done a lot of great work and I'm proud of my range. We were in the same Ironhack cohort. I want to grow into AI products. Can you tell me how I should approach that? Appreciate it.
note: Leads with self; connection buried; demanding ask. Low connection_warmth.

draft: Hi Jordan, fellow Columbia alum! I'm a huge fan of your amazing work and your incredible company — truly inspiring stuff, honestly the best in the industry. You seem super successful and smart. I'd love to pick your brain about, well, everything! Would you have some time to connect soon? You're awesome, thanks!
note: Empty flattery, no concrete detail, vague ask. Low personalization.
